note: NEGATIVE for the Pareto goal. bf16 rerank store (top 16 bits of f32, query kept full precision) to halve the rerank random-gather bandwidth. No QPS gain (+1.4% @C=1000, -1% @C=2000, -3.4% @C=4000) AND recall drops ~0.4pts (0.9990->0.9945 @C=2000). Why: the per-element bf16->f32 decode (shift) offsets the halved bytes, and the binary scan is still the larger share of the tiled funnel; the 7-bit mantissa costs recall. Net: sacrifices recall for ~no speed. (It IS a memory win: rerank store 3.9GB->1.95GB.)
